name: opencode-federal-platform-pack
description: OpenCode platform pack: repo review, solution architect discovery, platform design, GitOps audit, federal checklist, quality gate. Executable, evidence-based, enterprise-grade.

solution-architect.description: Discovery, requirements, constraints, high-level design; asks clarifying questions

product-manager-discovery.description: Turns vague ideas into scoped deliverables with acceptance criteria and estimates

repo-auditor.description: Reviews repos for architecture, maintainability, security, and deployment readiness

federal-security-reviewer.description: Federal-grade review: NIST 800-53, FedRAMP, FISMA, DoD-style controls

gitops-reviewer.description: Evaluates GitOps, CI/CD, IaC, observability, and platform engineering maturity

cloud-platform-reviewer.description: Evaluates AWS, Azure, GCP Well-Architected best practices

documentation-writer.description: Identifies documentation drift; proposes doc updates to match code/config/deploy

repo-assess.description: Full repository assessment: architecture, security, deployment readiness
repo-assess.template: Perform a comprehensive repository assessment. Use skills/well-architected-review. Workflow: (1) repo discovery — structure, entry points, tech stack; (2) architecture inference — components, dependencies; (3) security review — secrets, deps, supply chain; (4) observability review — logs, metrics, health; (5) scoring engine — 0–10 per category; (6) final report. Output: schemas/review-score.schema.json. Categories: security 0.25, reliability 0.20, performance 0.15, cost_awareness 0.10, operational_excellence 0.30. Include: review_target, summary (final_score, letter_grade, confidence, production_readiness), categories with score, rationale, evidence_found, missing_evidence, risks, recommended_actions, findings by severity, top_priorities. Blocking: none. Definition of done: report conforms to schema; all findings cite evidence or state missing_evidence.

solution-discovery.description: Solution architect discovery: requirements, budget, constraints, compliance
solution-discovery.template: Conduct solution architect discovery. Use contexts/solution-discovery-question-bank.md. Ask clarifying questions about requirements, budget, timeline, users, compliance, and integrations. Produce discovery summary with open questions and recommended next steps. Output: structured markdown. Blocking: none. Definition of done: discovery summary with assumptions, constraints, open questions. Context: $ARGUMENTS

platform-design.description: Platform architecture design with tradeoffs and cloud alignment
platform-design.template: Design platform architecture. Use instructions/aws-derived-principles.md. Workflow: (1) discovery — constraints from context; (2) constraints synthesis — scale, compliance, cost; (3) reference architecture — 1–3 options; (4) decision log — tradeoffs, risks. Output: Executive Summary, Architecture Score (0–10 per pillar), Key Risks, Evidence/Missing Evidence, Recommended Actions. If design would fail Well-Architected review, flag it. Blocking: none. Definition of done: 1–3 options with tradeoffs; risks and evidence documented. Context: $ARGUMENTS

federal-checklist.description: Federal compliance checklist (NIST, FedRAMP, FISMA, DoD)
federal-checklist.template: Produce federal compliance checklist. Use skills/federal-platform-review, skills/nist-compliance-evaluator, skills/well-architected-review; for AWS also skills/aws-federal-grade-checklist. Workflow: (1) evidence extraction — repo artifacts; (2) control-family mapping — NIST 800-53; (3) readiness report — gaps, evidence, remediation. Output: schemas/review-score.schema.json (primary) and schemas/compliance-report.json. Include: review_target, summary, categories, findings, top_priorities; control mapping in compliance extension. Blocking: none. Definition of done: control mapping; gaps with evidence; remediation roadmap. No certification claims.

gitops-audit.description: GitOps, CI/CD, and platform engineering maturity audit
gitops-audit.template: Audit GitOps, CI/CD, IaC, observability. Use skills/gitops-capability-audit, skills/well-architected-review. Evaluate 7 capability areas: CI/CD orchestration, GitOps and config-as-code, security scanning and supply chain, promotion and release governance, observability, identity and secrets, policy-as-code. Output: schemas/review-score.schema.json. Include: review_target, summary, categories (map GitOps capabilities to Well-Architected pillars), findings by severity, top_priorities. Blocking: none. Definition of done: findings per capability; evidence; remediation roadmap.

quality-gate.description: Pre-push quality gate: evidence, security, docs, supply chain
quality-gate.template: Run pre-push quality gate. Use docs/quality-gate-workflow.md, plugins/governance-plugin/rules-map.md, schemas/quality-gate.schema.json. FAIL if: plaintext secrets; :latest in prod; disabled TLS/permissive IAM; dep/CI/container/IaC change without security review; dep change without lock file; meaningful code/config/deploy change without doc update; recommendation without evidence. WARN if: new code without tests; architecture change without ADR; runbook not updated; behavior change without CHANGELOG; cloud resources without tags. Output: verdict (pass | pass_with_warnings | fail), blockers, warnings, informational, required_actions. Blocking: verdict fail blocks push readiness. Definition of done: verdict and findings conform to schema.

doc-sync.description: Identify documentation drift; propose doc updates to match code/config/deploy
doc-sync.template: Identify documentation drift. Use skills/well-architected-review for context; instructions/documentation-rules.md. Compare README, runbooks, architecture docs against code, config, IaC, CI. Output: drift report — file, section, drift type (outdated, missing, contradictory); proposed updates — file, section, proposed change, rationale; priority (critical, high, medium, low). Blocking: none. Definition of done: drift report with proposed updates; no implementation. Context: $ARGUMENTS

verify.description: Quick verification: quality-gate checks and status
verify.template: Run verification checks. Use docs/quality-gate-workflow.md, schemas/quality-gate.schema.json. Focused pass: (1) staged/unstaged changes; (2) security — secrets, :latest, lock files; (3) docs for meaningful changes; (4) evidence citations in review output. Output: verdict (pass | pass_with_warnings | fail), blockers, warnings, next steps. Be concise. Blocking: verdict fail blocks push readiness. Definition of done: verdict and findings.

checkpoint.description: Capture session state, decisions, and next steps for handoff
checkpoint.template: Create checkpoint for session handoff. Summarize: (1) current task and status; (2) key decisions made; (3) files modified or in progress; (4) blockers or dependencies; (5) recommended next steps. Output: structured markdown for new agent or human. Blocking: none. Definition of done: checkpoint with all five sections. Context: $ARGUMENTS

orchestrate.description: Plan multi-step workflow (review → design → federal check) with dependencies
orchestrate.template: Orchestrate multi-step platform engineering workflow. Given the goal, produce ordered plan with steps, dependencies, suggested commands. Example flows: repo-assess → platform-design → federal-checklist; solution-discovery → platform-design → quality-gate; gitops-audit → repo-assess → quality-gate. For each step: command, inputs, expected output, next-step trigger. Output: numbered plan with dependencies. Blocking: none. Definition of done: plan with at least 2 steps. Goal: $ARGUMENTS
